◈ The Working Developer's Algorithms Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide · Patterns & Use-Cases

The Working Developer's Algorithms

Not a leetcode grind — a tour of the dozen algorithmic ideas that quietly run real software, each paired with the scenarios where you'd reach for it. Learn the pattern, recognize it in the wild.

written for the modern developer · intuition first, then the code, then where it ships

▶ Start reading ⚡ Cheatsheet

Table of Contents

  1. Thinking About Cost — Big-O The one mental model behind every choice in this book. foundation
  2. Binary Search & "Search the Answer" Halving the haystack — and a trick that's far bigger than arrays. search
  3. Hashing & Hash Maps O(1) lookups: dedup, counting, caching, indexing. core
  4. Sorting — What Your Stdlib Really Does Timsort, pdqsort & why you almost never write your own. core
  5. Two Pointers & Sliding Window Turn O(n²) scans into O(n) — streams, ranges, rate limits. core
  6. Graphs: BFS & DFS Dependencies, crawlers, "is everything connected?" graph
  7. Shortest Paths: Dijkstra Cheapest route through a weighted world — maps, networks. graph
  8. Dynamic Programming Remember subproblems: diffs, autocomplete, edit distance. optimize
  9. Greedy & Intervals When grabbing the local best wins — scheduling, packing. optimize
  10. Heaps & Top-K Leaderboards, schedulers, "biggest N in a firehose." scale
  11. Probabilistic Structures Bloom filters & HyperLogLog: answers at scale, in kilobytes. scale
◈
About this book

Every chapter follows the same shape: the idea in plain language, a small worked example, the complexity, and a "where you'll meet it" panel of real scenarios. Self-contained and offline. Verified current as of mid-2026. Press ⌘R or reopen to refresh after edits.

⌂ Library
